title: Converge executable drift in scripts and tests

sources.reason: Defines the Cockpit workflow used to verify executable cleanup work items.
Implements the bandit low-risk baseline checker that needs direct coverage.
Verifies the Make targets that expose the executable helper.
Adds direct regression coverage for the bandit baseline helper.
Provides adjacent executable-helper coverage for the supply-chain scripts.

riskAssessment.reason: The cleanup is limited to a single executable helper and regression tests.

executionDecision.reason: The helper and its tests are now scoped narrowly enough to implement.

preReviewWarnings: Keep the lane limited to executable scripts and tests.
Verify the Makefile target still routes to scripts/check_bandit_baseline.py.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record a checkpoint before finishing the executable cleanup lane.

acceptance: tests/test_bandit_baseline.py covers the helper logic in scripts/check_bandit_baseline.py.
tests/test_makefile.py verifies the check-bandit-baseline Make target still routes to scripts/check_bandit_baseline.py.
python3 scripts/check_bandit_baseline.py exits successfully against the repository baseline.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The bandit baseline helper has no direct regression coverage, so the executable cleanup lane should add tests that lock in its current behavior and Makefile exposure.
constraints: Keep the cleanup limited to executable scripts and tests.
Do not broaden this lane into docs, README files, or workflow metadata.
Preserve the helper behavior while adding regression coverage.
intent.rationale: A narrow executable cleanup can lock in the baseline workflow without mixing in unrelated documentation or distribution changes.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
